description: A laid-back 12-hole course carved out of rural DeSoto County, operating today as Paradise Golf Course. Originally opened in 1971, the par-42 layout covers 3,592 yards and offers an unhurried round well suited to beginners and families. One of the few 12-hole courses in Florida.
notes: Course is now operated as Paradise Golf Course; 12-hole par-42 executive-style layout.

description: One of the oldest continuously operated municipal courses in Florida, Arcadia Municipal opened on Christmas Day 1922 with a handful of holes and grew into an 18-hole, par-72 layout. The open Bermuda fairways and sloping greens reflect an old-Florida feel that has drawn local golfers for over a century. Listed on the Florida Historic Golf Trail.
notes: On Florida Historic Golf Trail; 18-hole par-72 public municipal course.

description: Tucked inside the historic Gasparilla Inn & Club on Boca Grande Island, this Pete Dye-redesigned (2004) 18-hole course originally opened in 1929 and remains one of Southwest Florida's most picturesque island layouts. Access is limited to resort guests and club members. The course plays 6,836 yards from the tips at par 72.
notes: Resort/member-only access; Pete Dye redesign 2004; original course opened 1929.

description: A local favorite in northwest Bradenton, Pinebrook Ironwood is an 18-hole, par-61 executive course designed by Dean Refram and opened in 1985. Measuring 3,752 yards from the tips, it rewards precision over power and draws a loyal following of year-round players. Semi-private with public tee times available.
notes: Par-61 executive course; 18 holes; semi-private with public tee times.

description: Bradenton Country Club has served as the anchor of the local golf scene since its founding in 1924, making it one of the oldest private clubs on Florida's Gulf Coast. The 18-hole layout at 4646 Ninth Avenue West is members-only and steeped in regional history. A full-service private club with tennis and dining.
notes: Founded 1924; private members-only club.

description: Named for Jack Nicklaus's legendary concession putt to Tony Jacklin in the 1969 Ryder Cup, The Concession opened in 2006 as Golf Digest's Best New Private Course and consistently ranks among Florida's top ten. The 18-hole, par-72 championship layout spanning 520 acres was co-designed by Nicklaus and Jacklin, a pairing that never occurred on any other project. Extremely exclusive and invitation-only.
notes: Co-designed by Jack Nicklaus and Tony Jacklin; Golf Digest Best New Private Course 2006; ultra-exclusive private club.

description: A county-owned public facility operated by Manatee County since 1977, Manatee Golf Course sits at 6415 53rd Avenue West and provides accessible, affordable play for area residents. The 18-hole layout is open to all without a membership requirement. A reliable year-round option on Florida's Gulf Coast.
notes: County-owned public course; 18-hole.

description: Set within the gated Esplanade at Lakewood Ranch master-planned community, this 18-hole championship course opened around 2011 and is managed by Troon. Access is bundled with homeownership in the 1,250-home development, giving golf-deeded residents unlimited play. A resort-style private club with pools, spa, and fitness facilities.
notes: Bundled private course tied to home ownership; Troon-managed; city is Bradenton (zip 34211 is Lakewood Ranch area).

description: A 27-hole Arthur Hills-designed championship facility built in 2006 within the Heritage Harbour master-planned community, River Strand is tied to golf-deeded home ownership. The three nine-hole courses offer varied terrain along the Manatee River corridor, and the club hosts an active tournament calendar for its member-residents.
notes: 27-hole Arthur Hills design; private bundled-golf community; membership tied to golf-deeded homes.

description: Waterlefe Golf & River Club opened in 2000 along the Manatee River, featuring an 18-hole Ted McAnlis design that weaves through natural preserve land. The semi-private club offers annual Passport memberships to both community residents and non-residents, blending a private-club experience with selective outside access. Golf Digest has consistently recognized it among the area's top courses.
notes: Semi-private; Ted McAnlis design 2000; annual memberships available to non-residents.

description: Located on the edge of the Tara community in east Bradenton, The Preserve Golf Club at Tara opened in 2001 as a semi-public companion to the adjacent private Tara Golf & Country Club. The 18-hole layout accepts daily fee play with no membership required. A well-maintained course with a relaxed atmosphere.
notes: Semi-private with public tee times; opened 2001; operates as The Preserve Golf Club at Tara.

description: Tara Golf & Country Club is a bundled equity community where golf membership conveys with every deed, ensuring a true private-club experience for all 1,023 property owners. The 18-hole championship course opened in 1989 and the club hosted the Sarasota LPGA Classic in its early years. A mature, tree-lined layout in east Bradenton.
notes: Bundled private equity club; membership deeded with property; opened 1989.

description: River Isles is a compact 9-hole, par-30 executive course opened in 1968 and nestled inside a 55+ residential community along State Road 64. Water comes into play on seven of the nine holes, rewarding careful shot-making over distance. Walk-on play only; no tee times taken, with nominal green fees for outside visitors.
notes: 9-hole par-30 executive course; 55+ community; walk-on only; semi-private.

description: Myakka Pines is a member-owned semi-private club featuring a distinctive 27-hole layout that opened in 1978 on the banks of the Myakka River in Englewood. The three nine-hole combinations offer varied terrain through natural Florida scrub and wetlands. Pay-as-you-play is available, making it accessible beyond its membership base.
notes: 27-hole semi-private member-owned course; pay-as-you-play available.

description: Lemon Bay Golf Club is an Audubon-certified 18-hole, par-71 private course in Englewood that completed a major renovation by architect Drew Rogers in 2023. Originally opened around 1981 and set on 90 acres, the course is exclusive to members with limited summer public access. One of the more secluded and environmentally minded layouts on the Gulf Coast.
notes: Private with limited summer public access; major renovation completed 2023 by Drew Rogers; Audubon certified.

description: A par-60 executive course designed by Ted McAnlis and opened in 1990, Oyster Creek offers 18 holes within a gated community setting in Englewood. The layout plays to 3,976 yards and features six par-4s and twelve par-3s, making for quick and approachable rounds. Open to daily fee players with no membership required.
notes: Par-60 executive course; 18 holes; Ted McAnlis design 1990; public daily fee.

description: Key Royale Club occupies a prime waterfront site on Anna Maria Island in Holmes Beach, where the 9-hole executive course has been part of the community fabric since 1960. The compact layout provides residents and members a quick round with scenic island surroundings. Primarily a residential club with golf, tennis, and social amenities.
notes: 9-hole executive course on Anna Maria Island; established circa 1960.

description: King's Dunes is one of four championship courses at the private Lakewood Ranch Golf & Country Club, opened in 2005 with design credits to the Arnold Palmer organization. The fescue-toned bunker styling and links-inspired bunkering differentiate it from its sister courses. Access is reserved for members of the club and their guests.
notes: Part of Lakewood Ranch Golf & Country Club's four-course private campus; Arnold Palmer design.

description: Lakewood National opened in 2016 as an Arnold Palmer-designed 36-hole facility and quickly became the home of the Korn Ferry Tour's LECOM Suncoast Classic. The tournament-quality greens and aggressive bunker lines set it apart from other new-build courses in the region. Semi-private with bundled memberships for Lakewood National community residents.
notes: Host of the Korn Ferry Tour LECOM Suncoast Classic; Arnold Palmer design; 36 holes; semi-private bundled community.

description: The Ritz-Carlton Members Golf Club is a Tom Fazio-designed 18-hole, par-72 course spanning 315 acres in Lakewood Ranch, opened in 2006. Membership is restricted to select applicants and guests of the Ritz-Carlton Sarasota, maintaining an exclusive ratio of members to course. The course holds Audubon Cooperative Sanctuary status.
notes: Tom Fazio design 2006; private members and Ritz-Carlton Sarasota hotel guests only; Audubon sanctuary.

description: Cypress Links was the first course built at Lakewood Ranch Golf & Country Club, opening in 2001 with design credits to Arnold Palmer and Victoria Martz. It anchors the private club's 72-hole campus and features open, links-influenced fairways suited to the Florida terrain. Membership is not limited to community residents.
notes: Arnold Palmer/Victoria Martz design 2001; first course on the Lakewood Ranch G&CC campus; private.

description: Lakewood Ranch Golf & Country Club is a 72-hole private campus spanning four championship courses — Cypress Links (2001), King's Dunes (2005), Royal Lakes, and Legacy — all managed by Heritage Golf Group. Membership is open to non-residents as well as homeowners within the broader Lakewood Ranch community. The 44,000-square-foot main clubhouse and Lodge complement the multi-course experience.
notes: 72-hole private campus; 4 courses; Heritage Golf Group managed; membership not restricted to community residents.

The Lake Club Amenity Center is a residential clubhouse/amenity center with no on-site golf course. Golf access is provided through nearby Lakewood Ranch Golf & Country Club. Not a golf course facility; recommend skip.

description: Esplanade at Azario opened in 2020 as an 18-hole, par-72 championship course designed by Chris Wilczynski within the Lennar-developed Azario community in Lakewood Ranch. Golf memberships are bundled with golf-deeded homes, though the course also accepts outside tee times. Playing to over 7,000 yards, it is one of the newer championship layouts in the region.
notes: Chris Wilczynski design; opened May 2020; bundled golf-deeded community with limited outside play.

description: Harbourside at Longboat Key Club consists of three nine-hole courses — Red Hawk, White Egret, and Blue Heron — designed by Willard Byrd and opened in 1982, then renovated by Ron Garl in 2005. Set on a barrier island, the course is available to club members and guests staying at the Opal Collection's Longboat Key Club resort. A rare combination of private-club conditioning in a resort setting.
notes: Three 9-hole courses; Willard Byrd design 1982; Ron Garl renovation 2005; semi-private resort/member access.

description: IMG Academy Golf Club traces its roots to 1972, originally built as a private country club and later redeveloped by golf architect Gene Bates in 2003 as part of the world-renowned IMG Academy sports campus in Bradenton. The 18-hole, par-72 layout at 4350 El Conquistador Parkway is open to the public and frequently hosts collegiate and junior tournaments. One of the few courses in Florida with a professional sports training facility on-site.
notes: City corrected from Manatee County to Bradenton; Mark McCumber original 1972, Gene Bates renovation 2003; public.

description: A Ron Garl-designed public championship course opened in 1988, Buffalo Creek plays to 6,926 yards from the tips and is widely regarded as one of the best-value public courses in Manatee County. Located at 8100 69th Street East in Palmetto, it offers open fairways and strategic bunkering typical of Garl's Florida work.
notes: City corrected from Manatee County to Palmetto; Ron Garl design 1988; public championship course.

description: River Run Golf Links is a municipal par-70 course owned by the City of Bradenton, opened in 1987 along the Braden River. The 18-hole layout at 1801 27th Street East is one of the most affordable public options in Manatee County. No frills, but reliable conditions and easy access make it a staple for local players.
notes: City corrected from Manatee County to Bradenton; municipal par-70 public course; opened 1987.

description: Calusa Lakes Golf Club opened in 1991 in Nokomis, offering an 18-hole semi-private layout accessible via public tee times or the area's popular tri-club membership that includes Capri Isles and Waterford Golf Clubs. Set just off I-75 between Sarasota and Venice, it provides a convenient option for traveling golfers. Mature landscaping and water features define the visual character of the course.
notes: Semi-private; part of tri-club membership with Capri Isles and Waterford; public tee times available.

description: Mission Valley Golf & Country Club has operated as a private, member-owned community since 1967, one of the older established clubs between Sarasota and Venice. The 18-hole course at 1851 Mission Valley Boulevard winds through a residential setting with native Florida landscaping. Social, tennis, and dining amenities complement the golf.
notes: Private member-owned club; established 1967.

description: One of Southwest Florida's newest regulation courses, Wellen Park Golf & Country Club opened in February 2023 with an 18-hole, par-72 Gordon Lewis design playing to approximately 7,000 yards. Located within the master-planned Wellen Park community in North Port, the course is open for daily fee play with no membership required. A modern layout with wide fairways and tournament-ready infrastructure.
notes: Opened February 2023; Gordon Lewis design; public daily fee; newest regulation course in the region.

description: The Cove of Rotonda Golf Center is an 18-hole, par-59 executive course on the Cape Haze Peninsula, notable as the only lighted golf facility in the Port Charlotte–Englewood area. Opened around 1999, the 3,630-yard layout features elevated, well-bunkered greens and a lighted aqua driving range for evening play. Open to daily fee players without membership.
notes: Only lighted course in Port Charlotte/Englewood area; par-59 executive; public.

description: Maple Leaf Golf & Country Club is an 18-hole, par-62 executive course within a resident-owned 55+ manufactured home community, originally built in 1976. The 3,871-yard layout was later refined by Gordon Lewis and others over the decades. Semi-private with memberships available to non-residents.
notes: 55+ resident-owned community; par-62 executive course; semi-private with non-resident memberships available.

description: Kings Gate Golf Club is a semi-private 18-hole course in Port Charlotte opened in 1989, offering a casually elegant experience for both members and visiting daily fee players. The course at 24000 Rampart Boulevard plays through a mature residential community with tree-lined fairways. A straightforward and well-regarded layout for the Charlotte County market.
notes: Semi-private; daily fee play welcome; opened 1989.

description: Twin Isles Country Club has been a private anchor of the Punta Gorda golf community since its course opened in 1973, with the formal club structure established around 1985. Set just minutes from historic downtown Punta Gorda at 301 Madrid Boulevard, it is a members-first facility currently accepting a limited number of non-member rounds while it fills its roster. A classic Florida private club experience.
notes: Course opened 1973; formal club circa 1985; private with limited non-member play while filling membership.

description: Seminole Lakes Golf & Country Club is a Ron Garl-designed 18-hole course opened in 1992 at 26200 Stillwater Circle in Punta Gorda. The par-62 layout plays to 4,525 yards and is open to public daily fee play. This entry and the entry id a03d2a67 appear to reference the same single facility.
notes: POSSIBLE DUPLICATE with id a03d2a67-69ca-430f-a6ab-31f6097dc73c — both appear to be Seminole Lakes Golf & Country Club, 26200 Stillwater Circle, Punta Gorda FL 33955; Ron Garl design 1992; public.

description: Deep Creek Golf Club is a Mark McCumber-designed 18-hole course that opened in 1985, two miles off I-75 in the heart of Punta Gorda. The 6,000-yard, par-72 layout accepts daily fee play at affordable rates, bridging the gap between a private-club atmosphere and public access. A semi-private facility with a loyal local following.
notes: Mark McCumber design 1985; semi-private with daily fee play; 6,000 yards par-72.

description: Seminole Lakes Golf & Country Club is a Ron Garl-designed 18-hole course opened in 1992 at 26200 Stillwater Circle, Punta Gorda. The par-62 layout plays to 4,525 yards and is open to public daily fee play. This entry and the entry id 70604673 appear to reference the same single facility.
notes: LIKELY DUPLICATE with id 70604673-5135-4ddb-b5fb-d02416fa3393 — both appear to be Seminole Lakes Golf & Country Club, same address; recommend merging or marking one as duplicate.

description: Blue Heron Pines is a par-60, 18-hole executive course within a Sun Communities 55+ residential park in Punta Gorda, opened in 1993. Playing to 3,180 yards from the back tees, it emphasizes approachable golf with daily fee rates and membership options. Open to the public.
notes: Par-60 executive course; 55+ Sun Communities park; public with membership options; 18 holes.

description: The Hills is the original course at Rotonda Golf & Country Club, a design attributed to Jim Petrides that dates to the early development of Rotonda West in the 1970s. The 18-hole, par-72 layout at 100 Rotonda Circle is open to public daily fee play and represents the most historic of the three Rotonda courses. Gently rolling terrain and native vegetation define the round.
notes: Part of Rotonda Golf & Country Club; cityCorrection applied to Rotonda West; public daily fee; opened circa 1972.

description: The Palms is Rotonda Golf & Country Club's second 18-hole course, designed by D.J. DeVictor and opened around 1980. Playing to championship length through the coastal scrub landscape of Rotonda West, it offers public daily fee access and a more open character than the tree-lined Hills course. Both courses share the same main facility at 100 Rotonda Circle.
notes: Part of Rotonda Golf & Country Club; cityCorrection applied to Rotonda West; D.J. DeVictor design circa 1980; public.

description: Long Marsh is the newest of Rotonda Golf & Country Club's three courses, a Ted McAnlis design that opened in February 1999 across 450 acres of natural Florida scrub at 20 White Marsh Road. The 18-hole layout meanders through wetlands and preserves with a minimalist routing that keeps native habitat intact. Open to public daily fee play.
notes: Part of Rotonda Golf & Country Club complex; cityCorrection applied to Rotonda West; Ted McAnlis design 1999; public.

description: Bent Tree Country Club opened in 1975 as a fully private club and is best known for hosting the Sarasota LPGA Classic from 1976 to 1988, where Nancy Lopez claimed her first and 35th career victories. Designed by Bill Lewis, the 18-hole, par-72 course transitioned to semi-private in recent years, allowing outside daily fee play. Five sets of tees accommodate golfers of all skill levels.
notes: Hosted Sarasota LPGA Classic 1976–1988; Nancy Lopez won here; semi-private; Bill Lewis design.

description: Palm Aire Country Club is a Dick Wilson-designed 36-hole private equity club that opened in 1958, making it one of the older established golf communities in the Sarasota area. The two 18-hole championship courses are member-owned and member-governed, though the club welcomes outside play. Located at 5601 Country Club Way near the Sarasota-Manatee county line.
notes: Dick Wilson design 1958; 36-hole private equity club; semi-private access welcoming outside play.

description: Tatum Ridge Golf Links is a public 18-hole course in east Sarasota opened in 1989, offering an affordable and accessible daily-fee option away from the coast's private club corridors. The layout at 421 Tatum Road North plays through open terrain with multiple tee options for different skill levels. No membership required.
notes: Public daily fee; 18-hole; opened 1989.

description: Village Green Golf Club is an 18-hole, par-58 executive course built on a former orange grove in the Sarasota urban core, originally opened around 1970 and designed by William B. Lewis. At 3500 Pembrook Drive, it remains one of the few walkable, affordable golf options inside the city. Open to the public with no tee time reservations required.
notes: Par-58 executive course on former orange grove; William B. Lewis design circa 1970; public walkable.

description: Boca Royale Golf & Country Club is a semi-private course originally established in 1966 within a 1,000-acre gated community in Englewood on the Sarasota–Charlotte county border. Members and daily fee players are both welcome on the 18-hole layout, though range access requires membership. A newly redeveloped Neal Communities residential expansion surrounds the historic course.
notes: Semi-private; established 1966; Neal Communities residential expansion; range is members-only.

description: Jacaranda West Country Club is a semi-private 18-hole, par-72 course in Venice that opened in 1975, listed by the Venice Chamber of Commerce under semi-private golf facilities. The 6,589-yard layout at 1901 Jacaranda Boulevard welcomes both members and daily fee players. A straightforward, well-regarded course in south Sarasota County.
notes: Semi-private; Venice Chamber of Commerce classified; par-72; 6,589 yards; opened 1975.

description: Lake Venice Golf Club is a municipally operated public course on Venice Island, designed by Mark Mahannah and opened in 1959. The 18-hole layout at 1801 South Harbor Drive operates on a walk-on basis with no tee time reservations required, keeping it accessible to all skill levels. One of the oldest continuously operated municipal courses in Sarasota County.
notes: Municipal public course on Venice Island; no tee times taken; walk-on play; Mark Mahannah design 1959.

description: Pelican Pointe Golf & Country Club is a 27-hole semi-private non-equity club in Venice, opened in 1995 within a gated residential community. The three nine-hole combinations at 499 Derbyshire Drive accommodate members and daily fee visitors alike, with the club also popular as a wedding and event venue. A well-maintained course with broad appeal in the Venice golf market.
notes: 27-hole semi-private non-equity; opened 1995; also operates as event/wedding venue.

description: Sarasota National Golf Club is a Gordon Lewis-designed 18-hole, par-72 course opened in 2008 within the Sarasota National master-planned community in Venice. At 7,344 yards it plays as one of the longer tests in the region, and the club is managed by Troon. Golf memberships are bundled with golf-village homes while lake-village homeowners receive social memberships; limited outside tee times are available.
notes: Gordon Lewis design 2008; Troon-managed; bundled golf community; 7,344 yards par-72; limited outside play.
